images.altText: Women wearing medals place their heads together
Shadow of soldier playing last post reflected on wall

children.content: Australians paid tribute to those who have served at services and marches, but the solemn mood at Melbourne, Sydney and Perth Dawn Services was interrupted by boos during Welcome to Country addresses. Follow live.

items.title: Australians mark Anzac Day in solemn memorials as individuals heckling at services condemned

updates.title: Ode to the Fallen read by 11yo at Sydney Dawn Service
Veteran and Indigenous elder heckled during Welcome to Country in Perth
Tens of thousands at Melbourne shrine post-service

items.title: Indigenous solar projects to tackle diesel shortage concerns and halve power bills

children.content: Two remote Indigenous communities have attracted a combined $11 million in funding for solutions to tackle their fuel security and cost-of-living crises.

image.altText: A photo showing a person walking towards a tractor carrying solar panels surround by fence and car outside the fence

items.title: Former high-profile NT doctor to stand trial on rape, gross indecency charges

children.content: A former high-profile Northern Territory doctor charged with multiple sexual offences, including rape, has been committed to stand trial later this year.

image.altText: Picture of a doctor wearing a white lab coat with a stethoscope

items.title: 'Amazing' horse-led Anzac Day march tradition continues over two decades

children.content: Every year on Anzac Day, for more than two decades, Janette Wilson saddles up her Waler horse and leads the procession for the Darwin march, representing the light horsemen and their companions who served in World War I.

image.altText: A woman, smiling widely, standing next to a horse with a red poppy tucked behind its ear, in a horse pen.

items.title: Young stroke survivor works to raise awareness as NT falls behind

children.content: When Alison Reidy, then 38, woke in terrible pain, she had no idea she was having a stroke. Now she's trying to improve stroke awareness in the Northern Territory.

image.altText: A woman looking at the camera with a serious facial expression as she sits in front of gym equipment.

items.title: The 'internationally acclaimed' French sculptor who is nowhere to be found

children.content: An Australian gallery has been selling sculptures as the work of an "internationally acclaimed" French artist. But the Paris backstory doesn’t stack up.

gallery.altText: A man's silhouette reflected in a glass window in Paris. The Eiffel Tower is reflected in the window next to him.
A woman with short greying hair holds a crumbled blue sheet of plastic upright.

thumbnail.altText: A man's silhouette reflected in a glass window in Paris. The Eiffel Tower is reflected in the window next to him.
A woman with short greying hair holds a crumbled blue sheet of plastic upright.

items.title: One Nation's outcome in Farrer could be a watershed moment

children.content: The Coalition's preference deals in Farrer could help fracture the Liberal Party's moderate base and invigorate dissatisfied voters.

image.altText: David Farley speaks while reclining on a park bench.

items.title: James Valentine knew good radio and I was his wide-eyed apprentice

children.content: As an aspiring broadcaster, I covertly watched everything James Valentine did on air. He taught me how in the absurd minutia of our lives, we reveal ourselves to each other.

image.altText: a man behind a microphone looks into the camera

items.title: Beaconsfield mine rescue continues to defy belief two decades on

children.content: For a dramatic fortnight, the plight of two miners trapped deep underground in Tasmania's north captivated the world. Twenty years later, the Beaconsfield rescue continues to defy belief.

gallery.altText: LtoR Todd Russell and Brant Webb wave as they emerge from the mine lift

items.title: Dissecting the art of apologising, from MAFS men to Tucker Carlson

image.altText: Tucker Carlson with his arms crossed

avatar.alt: Virginia Trioli in a stylish suit on a set with lighting behind her

children.content: There's almost something weaponised about the contemporary apology, and perversely the social media culture that aims to teach self-awareness and accountability might just be responsible for it.

items.title: From arrests to resignations, the Epstein fallout one year on from Giuffre's death

children.content: Virginia Giuffre didn't live to witness the full impact of the truths she brought forward, but the arrests, resignations and the unravelling of reputations came in the wake of her decision to speak.

image.altText: A close up of a man's face with black out words around him

items.title: The chart that shows how much the NDIS price tag has blown out

children.content: An analysis of price forecasts in previous budgets reveals the NDIS was on track to cost more than four times what was initially expected.

image.altText: An NDIS logo is seen on a building

items.title: Leaked Pentagon email shows US wants to punish NATO allies over Iran war

children.content: The email suggests the US may review the UK's claim to the Falkland Islands and Spain's NATO membership as punishment over their stance on the Iran war.

image.altText: Falkland islanders celebrate referendum result.

items.title: The donkey marching for the memory of all animals that serve in war

children.content: Penny the donkey, adorned in bright purple poppies, is spreading the Anzac Day legacy not just in Australia, but also in Cheshire in England.

image.altText: A grey donkey face up close in a paddock

children.content: Director James Litchfield brings his experience growing up in rural Australia to his debut feature film, Alphabet Lane.

items.title: Inmates accused of sexual assault in prison continued to share cells, report finds

children.content: A Queensland Ombudsman report has found three instances where prison inmates accused of sexually assaulting cellmates have continued to share cells with other prisoners.

image.altText: A corridor inside a jail with locked doors.

items.title: 'Fear and hope' of foster care journey inspires teen artist

children.content: An Indigenous teenager is already making a name for herself as an artist, using her work to explore her identity and experiences in the care system.

image.altText: Keira creating an artwork at her kitchen table.

items.title: Family keeps century-old farming trade alive

children.content: Brothers in northern NSW continue a tool trade that's more than 100 years old. They believe they are some of the last manufacturers of ear-marking pliers in Australia.

items.title: Vic govt announces $860m for 7,000 extra social housing properties

children.content: An extra 7,000 social housing properties will be built in Victoria in the coming decade, with an initial $860 million investment in the state budget, the Allan government has announced.

image.altText: Social housing in Heidelberg West.

items.title: Anzac biscuit-tin guitars sold to aid veteran charity

children.content: A Tasmanian guitar maker will donate Anzac biscuit-tin guitars to his local RSL to raise money for veteran mental health charity.

image.altText: guitars made from biscuit tins and the man who makes them

items.title: Family history drives Wallaroos star in Anzac Day Test against NZ

children.content: Any time Piper Duck pulls on the Australian jersey is a special moment, but playing in the historic Anzac Day Test holds even greater significance for the Wallaroos back-rower.

items.title: Netanyahu delayed revealing prostate cancer diagnosis due to Iran war

children.content: Benjamin Netanyahu said a malignant tumour was discovered during a routine check-up and he has since undergone "targeted treatment".

image.altText: A besuited man speaks at a podium.

items.title: 'Homophobia has no place': AFL boss rejects ruling on slurs in sport

children.content: Both the AFL and the AFL Players' Association hit back at suggestions from the appeals board that slurs are commonplace among players in the sport.

items.title: Sinner urges Alcaraz not to rush back as injury ends French Open three-peat dream

children.content: Two-time defending champion Carlos Alcaraz will not be able to defend his titles at Roland Garros due to a wrist injury that has friend and rival Jannik Sinner concerned.

image.altText: Carlos Alcaraz hugs Jannik Sinner after the French Open final.

items.title: De Minaur thumped by teen in Madrid as rough clay season continues

children.content: Alex de Minaur loses another early-round match at the Madrid Masters, going down to local teen Rafael Jodar in straight sets as his clay court season struggles.

items.title: Surfer turns decommissioned wind turbine blades into surfboard fins
